channel.title: Robert Boles
channel.description: A practical knowledge base from Robert Boles on QA systems, automation, release confidence, and builder experiments.

item.title: It's Not Your Automation Tool That's Broken — It's Your Process
item.description: Teams blame the framework when nightly runs go red and tests get flaky. Most of the time the framework isn't the problem — nobody owns the suite. Here's what changes when you fix that instead of rewriting it.

item.title: Your QA Dashboard Doesn't Need More Charts, It Needs Fewer Questions
item.description: Most QA dashboards get built bottom-up from whatever the tools export, which is how they end up full of vanity metrics nobody acts on. The Goal-Question-Metric framework flips the build order.

item.title: Traceable Isn't the Same as True
item.description: A v1 launch scan on Vox Mana came back green — records existed, citations resolved, generated pages matched their source, regression passed. Exploratory testing found the checks were proving the wrong thing anyway. Here's the gap, and the rule I use to close it.

item.title: Your Precon Is a Passport
item.description: A precon isn't a lesser deck you apologize for — it's the fastest legal way into a Commander game. The nerves aren't the problem. Not having four sentences ready is.

item.title: Izzet and Prismari Are Both Blue-Red. They're Not the Same Deck.
item.description: Same two colors, same color pie, completely different game. Izzet and Prismari prove that a color identity tells you the toolbox, not which tool gets picked up twice a turn.

item.title: A Quiz Can Be Evidence, Not Vibes
item.description: Most fandom quizzes hand you a confident label and no receipts. Building voxmana.io's placement quiz, I built the other kind — one that tracks its evidence, resolves the near-miss, and refuses to call a vibe a fact.

item.title: Static Doesn’t Mean Safe — What Building voxmana.io Taught Me
item.description: Building voxmana.io as a static site didn't remove release risk — it hid it in the seams. Here's where a 'static' side project can still bite, and how I made those risks visible before launch.

item.title: How I Cut a Regression Cycle in Half
item.description: Our platform upgraded twice a year, and testing one upgrade in full took three months. Here's the method that cut it to six weeks: an honest audit, a coverage map, a business-built rubric, vendor guidance, and ISTQB-style risk scoring.

item.title: The Ages of Fixing: What Every Magic Land Is Charging You
item.description: Thirty years of Magic land design is one long argument about a single question: how easy should it be to cast your spells on time? Read the lands as answers and it starts to make sense.

item.title: How I Run AI Like a QA System
item.description: AI is non-deterministic by design. I run my AI work the way I run quality engineering: operating prompts, grounding, examples, gates, and feedback loops.

item.title: What Good QA Actually Owns
item.description: QA owns quality — and you own it by making it visible to the right people, in the language they can act on, before it ships.
